perturbation.original_text: 2.10 "Improvement" or "Improvements" means any modification of a process or other technology described in a Patent, and any modification to Technical Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.10 "Improvement" or "Improvements" means any modification of a process or other technology.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "described in a Patent, and any modification to Technical Information," the definition of 'Improvement' becomes ambiguous. This impacts Article 5, where the licensing of Improvements is discussed in relation to both Patents and Technical Information. The modified definition creates uncertainty regarding whether modifications to Technical Information are also considered Improvements, creating an in-text contradiction with Article 5 where Technical Information is considered Improvements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.00 Improvements made or acquired solely by the Company ("Company Improvements") during the term of this IP Agreement shall be deemed Technical Information hereunder and shall be subject to the license provisions set forth in Article III for Technical Information. Any patent applications and any patents relating to any Company Improvements shall be deemed Patents hereunder and shall be subject to the license provisions set forth in Article III for Patents.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Improvement' explicitly included 'modification of a process or other technology described in a Patent, and any modification to Technical Information'. Article 5 states that 'Company Improvements' are deemed 'Technical Information'. By omitting 'described in a Patent, and any modification to Technical Information' from the definition of 'Improvement', it creates ambiguity as to whether modifications to Technical Information are considered Improvements. Since Article 5 treats Improvements as Technical Information, the omission creates a conflict regarding whether Company Improvements are necessarily Improvements as defined in 2.10, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.02 If the Option is not exercised prior to the expiration of the Option Period, then Company agrees to extend the license grants set forth in Articles 3.00 and 3.01 beyond the expiration of the Option Period, subject to an annual licensing fee paid by Investor to Company. The non- exclusive rights granted to Investor under this Article 3.02 do not include the right to grant sublicenses to Third Parties. The amount and parameters of the annual licensing fee shall be commercially reasonable, as determined by an independent expert who is mutually agreeable to the Parties and whose determination shall be final and binding; provided, however, that if the Parties cannot agree on the independent expert, each Party shall designate an expert of their choice and the two experts designated by the Parties shall work together in good faith to identify and designate a third, independent expert whose determination shall be binding.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.02 If the Option is not exercised prior to the expiration of the Option Period, then Company agrees to extend the license grants set forth in Articles 3.00 and 3.01 beyond the expiration of the Option Period. The non- exclusive rights granted to Investor under this Article 3.02 do not include the right to grant sublicenses to Third Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the text about the annual licensing fee, a critical term concerning the continued licensing after the option period expires is removed. This omission creates an inconsistency because the initial statement says that the license will be extended if the Option isn't excercised, however the terms that allow this license to be extended in the text (Annual licensing fee, Independent Expert) are now removed. Without an agreement for an annual fee, there is no consideration for the contract extension, meaning if the Option is not excercised there is no guarentee that the licenses can be extended, making the first sentence in contradiction with the rest of the text
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.02 If the Option is not exercised prior to the expiration of the Option Period, then Company agrees to extend the license grants set forth in Articles 3.00 and 3.01 beyond the expiration of the Option Period. The non- exclusive rights granted to Investor under this Article 3.02 do not include the right to grant sublicenses to Third Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the annual licensing fee and independent expert determination. This omission directly contradicts the initial statement that the license will be extended if the option isn't exercised in Article 3.02, because the parameters that allow the license extension are removed. Without the fee, there's no guarantee of extension, causing a clear in-text contradiction, as described in the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition. The removal of essential terms that enable the extension of the license creates a direct conflict with the original statement that the license 'agrees to extend the license grants set forth in Articles 3.00 and 3.01 beyond the expiration of the Option Period.'

perturbation.original_text: 3.09 Company represents that to the best of its knowledge, the methods described and claimed in the Patents and the Technical Information, and the products produced thereby in accordance with such information, will be free from claims of infringement of the patents and copyrights of any Third Party. Company further represents that it has not received any written notice of a claim and otherwise has no knowledge that the methods described and claimed in the Patents and the Technical Information, and the products produced thereby in accordance with such information, violate or infringe upon the rights of any Person. Company shall indemnify and hold harmless Investor, Investors' Affiliates, and their officers and directors, and their direct and indirect customers, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, from and against any and all losses, liabilities, obligations, claims, contingencies, damages, diminution in value, deficiencies, actions, proceedings, taxes, costs and expenses, including all judgments, amounts paid in settlements, court costs and reasonable attorneys' fees and costs of investigation as incurred, arising out of or relating to any breach of any of the representations made in this Article 3.09.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.09 Company represents that to the best of its knowledge, the methods described and claimed in the Patents and the Technical Information, and the products produced thereby in accordance with such information, will be free from claims of infringement of the patents and copyrights of any Third Party. Company further represents that it has not received any written notice of a claim and otherwise has no knowledge that the methods described and claimed in the Patents and the Technical Information, and the products produced thereby in accordance with such information, violate or infringe upon the rights of any Person.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the indemnification clause from Article 3.09, an in-text contradiction is created with Article 6.00. Article 6.00 explicitly references the indemnification obligations under Article 3.09, but those obligations are now removed. This means Article 6.00 refers to a non-existent indemnification, causing confusion on the extent of Company's liabilities and Investor's protections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.00 SUBJECT TO AND EXCEPT FOR ANY INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS UNDER ARTICLE 3.09 ABOVE, NOTHING IN THIS IP AGREEMENT WILL BE DEEMED TO BE A REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY BY COMPANY OF THE ACCURACY, SAFETY OR USEFULNESS FOR ANY PURPOSE OF ANY TECHNICAL INFORMATION, TECHNIQUES, OR PRACTICES AT ANY TIME MADE AVAILABLE BY COMPANY. COMPANY WILL HAVE NO LIABILITY WHATSOEVER TO INVESTOR OR ANY OTHER PERSON FOR OR ON ACCOUNT OF ANY INJURY, LOSS OR DAMAGE OF ANY KIND OR NATURE, SUSTAINED BY, OR ANY DAMAGES ASSESSED OR ASSERTED AGAINST, OR ANY OTHER LIABILITY INCURRED BY OR IMPOSED ON INVESTOR OR ANY OTHER PERSON, ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH OR RESULTING FROM: (A) THE PRODUCTION, USE OR SALE OF ANY APPARATUS OR PRODUCT OR METHOD, OR THE PRACTICE OF THE PATENTS BY INVESTOR OR ITS ASSIGNS; (B) THE USE BY INVESTOR OR ITS ASSIGNS OF ANY TECHNICAL INFORMATION, TECHNIQUES, OR PRACTICES DISCLOSED BY COMPANY; OR (C) ANY ADVERTISING OR OTHER PROMOTIONAL ACTIVITIES BY INVESTOR OR ITS ASSIGNS WITH RESPECT TO ANY OF THE FOREGOING, AND INVESTOR WILL HOLD COMPANY, AND ITS OFFICERS, EMPLOYEES AND AGENTS, HARMLESS IN THE EVENT COMPANY, OR ITS OFFICERS, EMPLOYEES OR AGENTS, IS HELD LIABLE.
perturbation.justification: The original text included an indemnification clause in Article 3.09, which is then explicitly referenced in Article 6.00. The changed text omits this indemnification clause from Article 3.09, creating a direct contradiction with Article 6.00 because Article 6.00 now refers to an indemnification obligation that no longer exists. This qualifies as an 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' according to the provided definitions.
